population: ICU patients with sepsis
effect_size: AUROC nomogram vs SOFA and SAPSII
performance: Training set: AUROC nomogram vs SOFA Z=-5.0879 p<0.01; nomogram vs SAPSII Z=2.8677 p=0.004; Validation set: nomogram vs SOFA Z=-5.5984 p<0.01; nomogram vs SAPSII Z=2.7171 p=0.007
notes: Nomogram AUROC 0.746 vs SAPSII AUROC 0.664; nomogram had significantly better predictive accuracy than SAPSII and SOFA in both training and validation sets

source_info.section: **Logistic regression variable screening results and nomogram development**

population: Sepsis patients (training and validation sets)
method: NRI, IDI, Hosmer-Lemeshow test, calibration plots, stepwise logistic regression (AIC)
effect_size: NRI: 47.32% (95% CI: 36.08–70.46%) in training set; NRI: 36.42% (95% CI: 16.11–59.11%) in validation set; IDI significantly higher than SOFA and SAPSII in both sets
performance: Hosmer-Lemeshow: χ² = 11.89 (p=0.22) in training set; χ² = 12.13 (p=0.21) in validation set; calibration curves showed satisfactory conformity between predictions and observations in both sets
notes: Stepwise logistic regression AIC values reported for deleted variables including Fiberscope, Gender, PH, APTT, HEMOGLOBIN, WBC, Max CVP value, Vasopressor maximum Dose, PO2, Pneumomycosis, PT, Glucose, Breath rate, CHF, Neurologic disease, Aids, Diabetes, CVP, Oxygenation index, PCO2, Blood culture, PLATELET, COPD, ALBUMIN, TnT, BILIRUBIN. Bias-corrected and apparent calibration curves deviated only slightly from the reference line.
